summary: Exceptional test suite that mirrors the TDD cycle structure of the implementation. Coverage is comprehensive: all seven feature cycles (package.json parsing, requirements.txt parsing, LicenseConfig classification, single-dep license checking, report generation, mock lookup, and end-to-end integration) each have their own focused test class. Rigor is strong — edge cases are well represented: empty manifests, missing files, malformed JSON, bare package names without version specifiers, pip option lines, case-insensitive classification, lookup returning None, lookup throwing exceptions, unknown licenses flagging non-compliance alongside denied ones, and mixed-status reports. Design is excellent: tests are grouped logically by functional cycle with clear comments explaining the red/green/refactor context, a small reusable `_write_temp` / `_make_manifest` helper reduces boilerplate, `setUp` fixtures keep individual tests concise, `MagicMock` is used cleanly for injectable lookup functions, and all temp files are properly cleaned up in `finally` blocks ensuring test isolation. The one minor gap is the absence of tests for concurrent or multi-call mock behavior verification (e.g., asserting `mock_lookup.call_count`), but this is a very minor omission given the overall depth. Would confidently trust this suite to catch regressions.
